layout.structure: Single Page App with Dashboard Layout
layout.grid_system: grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-12 gap-6 h-screen max-h-screen overflow-hidden

areas.header: col-span-full h-16 flex items-center justify-between px-6 border-b bg-white/50 backdrop-blur-md z-10
areas.canvas_area: col-span-full lg:col-span-9 relative rounded-2xl overflow-hidden border shadow-sm bg-white
areas.sidebar_controls: col-span-full lg:col-span-3 flex flex-col gap-6 p-6 bg-white/50 rounded-2xl border shadow-sm h-full overflow-y-auto

buttons.primary: bg-primary text-white hover:bg-primary/90 shadow-lg hover:shadow-xl transition-all active:scale-95 rounded-full px-8 py-4 text-lg font-bold tracking-wide
buttons.secondary: bg-white text-foreground border border-input hover:bg-accent hover:text-accent-foreground shadow-sm rounded-lg px-4 py-2 text-sm font-medium transition-colors

inputs.slider: w-full h-2 bg-secondary rounded-lg appearance-none cursor-pointer accent-primary
inputs.number: bg-transparent border-b-2 border-muted focus:border-primary outline-none text-center font-mono text-lg w-20 transition-colors

cards.stat_card: bg-white p-4 rounded-xl border shadow-sm flex flex-col items-center justify-center gap-1

visual_enhancers.textures: Subtle dot grid pattern on canvas background (CSS radial-gradient)

animations.explosion: Particle system expansion and fade out

images.logo: Use text logo 'Physics Lab' with a simple geometric icon (circle/square)
images.sprites: Draw game elements (Tank, Cannon, Wall) using HTML5 Canvas API primitives (rect, arc, path) for crisp vector look. DO NOT use external images.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure text is Slate-900 on Slate-50 backgrounds. Use Slate-500 for secondary text.
accessibility.aria_labels: All sliders and buttons must have descriptive aria-labels

instructions_to_main_agent: Use 'Space Grotesk' from Google Fonts for headings if possible, otherwise fallback to sans-serif.
Implement the game loop using `requestAnimationFrame` for smooth 60fps physics.
Draw the 'Cannon' as a rotating rectangle on a semi-circle base.
Draw the 'Tank' as a combination of rectangles and a small barrel line.
Draw the 'Wall' as a solid rectangle with a striped pattern fill (context.createPattern).
Use a 'dashed' line style for the trajectory prediction.
The 'Fire' button should be the most prominent UI element in the sidebar.
Show 'Angle' and 'Velocity' values dynamically as the user drags sliders.
Implement a 'Reset' button to clear the canvas/trajectory.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur)
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: `transition: all`. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add `.App { text-align: center; }` in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯📚🎭🎬🎪🎉🎊🎁🎀🎂🍰🎈🎨🎰💰💵💳🏦💎🪙💸🤑📊📈📉💹🔢🏆🥇 etc for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories (especially sponsor logos) = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable
Create new components that match the style and conventions of existing components when needed
Examine existing components to understand the project's component patterns before creating new ones
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...)
Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...})
